name: the-complete-works-of-chuang-tzu
description: Chuang Tzu's foundational Taoist text — parables and philosophy on freedom from convention, spontaneous action, and merging with the Tao.
useCase: Understand Taoist philosophy through the parables and paradoxes of Chuang Tzu — freedom, spontaneity, and the relativiry of all things.
knownRisks: Ancient text with complex cultural and historical context
Multiple authorship — not all chapters convey the same philosophy
Some passages describe magical or supernatural powers that should be read metaphorically
